title: Remove unused `recovery-data` mount point
summary: The worker declares a `recovery-data` filesystem storage mounted at `/recovery-data` (worker/charmcraft.yaml:55-56 mount, :68-70 storage). The only thing that ever references `/recovery-data` is the coordinator's MimirConfig default `recovery_data_dir = Path('/recovery-data')` (coordinator/src/mimir_config.py:114), consumed solely by `_build_alertmanager_storage_config` to set alertmanager_storage.filesystem.dir = /recovery-data/data-alertmanager (mimir_config.py:191-195). But MimirConfig is instantiated at coordinator/src/charm.py:103 WITHOUT passing recovery_data_dir, and whenever S3 is ready (the normal coordinator deployment), `_update_s3_storage_config(mimir_config['alertmanager_storage'], 'alerts')` (mimir_config.py:151) POPS the entire `filesystem` block (mimir_config.py:339-341) and replaces it with a storage_prefix. So in any real (S3-backed) deployment the `/recovery-data` path never appears in the rendered config, and the mount/storage are dead weight.
why_good_first_issue: Small, well-bounded cleanup with an existing tracker issue and an explicit repro. The deletion is mechanical: drop the mount + storage from worker/charmcraft.yaml, and remove the now-unused recovery_data_dir plumbing (or repoint the alertmanager filesystem dir to root_data_dir) in mimir_config.py and its one unit test. Teaches a newcomer charmcraft.yaml metadata, the coordinator config builder, and how to update a unit test -- all without needing a live cluster to understand it.
repro_steps: multipass exec cos -- bash -lc 'cd ~/work/mimir-operators && grep -rn recovery . --include=*.py --include=*.yaml | grep -v /.git/' ; multipass exec cos -- bash -lc 'cd ~/work/mimir-operators && sed -n "44,72p" worker/charmcraft.yaml' ; multipass exec cos -- bash -lc 'cd ~/work/mimir-operators && sed -n "95,120p" coordinator/src/charm.py' (shows MimirConfig() omits recovery_data_dir) ; and sed -n "333,342p" coordinator/src/mimir_config.py (shows filesystem popped on S3).
repro_result: Confirmed by inspection at HEAD. All `recovery` references: worker/charmcraft.yaml:55-56 (mount /recovery-data), :68 (storage recovery-data); coordinator/src/mimir_config.py:114 (default `recovery_data_dir: Path = Path('/recovery-data')`), :119 (stored), :193 (`'dir': str(self._recovery_data_dir / 'data-alertmanager')`); and only the unit test coordinator/tests/unit/test_mimir_config.py:101. Call site coordinator/src/charm.py:103-108 instantiates MimirConfig WITHOUT recovery_data_dir, so default applies; but `_update_s3_storage_config` (mimir_config.py:151 -> :339-341) pops the `filesystem` key for alertmanager_storage whenever S3 is ready, so the /recovery-data path is absent from the rendered config in production -- matching the issue's repro (rendered config shows only /data/* paths, no /recovery-data). Hence the mount + storage are genuinely unused.
fix_hint: 1) Delete the mount (worker/charmcraft.yaml:55-56) and the `recovery-data:` storage entry (~:68-70). 2) In coordinator/src/mimir_config.py remove the `recovery_data_dir` parameter (:114) and `self._recovery_data_dir` (:119), and change `_build_alertmanager_storage_config` (:191-195) to use `self._root_data_dir / 'data-alertmanager'` (it gets overwritten by storage_prefix under S3 anyway). 3) Update coordinator/tests/unit/test_mimir_config.py:101 expectation from `/recovery-data/data-alertmanager` to `/data/data-alertmanager`. Run `tox -e unit` in coordinator/ to confirm.
notes: Open issue, no labels yet but textbook good-first-issue (originally filed under bug template; really a cleanup). Note the issue understates it: /recovery-data is referenced in code via the MimirConfig default, but is inert in production because S3 pops the filesystem block -- so the cleanup must also remove that dead plumbing + fix the unit test, not just the charmcraft.yaml mount. Verified at HEAD (depth-80 clone). Aside: mimir's requires-python='~=3.14.0' is correctly pinned (unlike loki-operators), so no specifier defect here.

title: Some nginx routes are missing (/compactor/ring, /ingester/ring, /store-gateway/ring)
summary: The nginx reverse-proxy in the coordinator does not expose the per-component ring status/healthcheck endpoints `/compactor/ring`, `/ingester/ring`, and `/store-gateway/ring`. In coordinator/src/nginx_config.py only `/ruler/ring` is wired (line 33). There is no `_locations_ingester` or `_locations_store_gateway` block at all, and `_locations_compactor` (line 50) contains only `/api/v1/upload/block/` -- no `/compactor/ring`. So requests to those ring pages through nginx do not route to their backends.
why_good_first_issue: The fix mirrors an existing, working example in the same file: the `/ruler/ring` NginxLocationConfig (nginx_config.py:33). A beginner adds analogous `NginxLocationConfig(path='/compactor/ring', backend='compactor', modifier='=')` entries for compactor/ingester/store-gateway and includes them in `server_ports_to_locations` (nginx_config.py:64-71). Self-contained, one file, pattern-to-copy already present. Easy to add a unit assertion that the new paths appear.
repro_steps: multipass exec cos -- bash -lc 'cd ~/work/mimir-operators && sed -n "20,120p" coordinator/src/nginx_config.py' ; grep -n "/ring" coordinator/src/nginx_config.py
repro_result: Confirmed by inspection at HEAD. coordinator/src/nginx_config.py contains exactly one `/ring` route: line 33 `NginxLocationConfig(path='/ruler/ring', backend='ruler', modifier='=')`. There is NO `/compactor/ring`, `/ingester/ring`, or `/store-gateway/ring`; `_locations_compactor` (:50-52) only defines `/api/v1/upload/block/`, and there are no ingester/store-gateway location blocks. `server_ports_to_locations` (:64-72) concatenates distributor+ruler+alertmanager+query_frontend+compactor only. So the three ring endpoints from the issue are still unrouted -> issue is still valid (not yet fixed).
fix_hint: Copy the `/ruler/ring` pattern (nginx_config.py:33). Add `NginxLocationConfig(path='/compactor/ring', backend='compactor', modifier='=')` to `_locations_compactor`; add new `_locations_ingester = [NginxLocationConfig(path='/ingester/ring', backend='ingester', modifier='=')]` and `_locations_store_gateway = [NginxLocationConfig(path='/store-gateway/ring', backend='store-gateway', modifier='=')]`; include both in the concatenation in `server_ports_to_locations`. Add a unit assertion that the new paths are present. NOTE the issue raises a design caveat (these ring pages have a 'Forget' button) -- a maintainer should confirm exposing them via ingress is desired before merging; flag in the PR.
notes: Open issue (no labels). Issue links the OLD mimir-coordinator-k8s-operator path; logic now lives in coordinator/src/nginx_config.py in this monorepo, re-verified there. Slightly less of a clean GFI than #25 due to the 'Forget'-button ingress design question the issue itself raises, but the code change is trivial and pattern-driven. Verified at HEAD (depth-80 clone).
